key_points: Agriculture is closely linked with industrial development
Plantation agriculture requires large capital investment and labour
Technological reforms introduced HYV seeds, fertilizers, and irrigation
The Green Revolution increased food grain production in India
Sustainable agriculture focuses on protecting soil and water resources
examples: Green Revolution improved wheat production in Punjab and Haryana
Tea plantations require processing factories near plantations
Cotton is a major raw material for the textile industry
Pulses improve soil fertility through nitrogen fixation
Rubber plantations are common in Kerala due to humid climate
formulas: Agricultural Development = Technology + Irrigation + Institutional Reforms
Food Security = Crop Production + Efficient Distribution
exam_tips: Link agriculture with industries in answers
Explain reasons behind crop distribution patterns
Use examples of reforms like the Green Revolution
Discuss sustainability when writing long answers
